attacking_style: Specializes in opportunistic late-race attacks, typically launching inside final 3km or even sub-500m when sprint teams are disorganized or exhausted. Favors surprise timing over sustained climbing attacks.
team_tactics: Cofidis employs him in speculative breakaway gambits even when significant time down, forcing rivals to expend domestique resources. Operates with autonomy to seize late-race chaos rather than structured leadouts.
terrain_preference: Circuit races and stages with chaotic finales where sprint teams fragment. Struggles on pure descending speed despite KOM ability. Best when technical positioning trumps raw power.
vulnerability: Tactical hesitation in final kilometer when in winning position. Lacks descending speed to capitalize on climbing gains. Ineffective when peloton maintains high, organized speeds into finales.
key_patterns: Launches surprise attacks sub-1km when sprint teams disorganized, nearly stealing stages. (2 instances)
Shows KOM climbing ability but cannot convert on technical descents against specialists. (2 instances)
Teams force rival resource commitment through speculative breakaway presence despite time deficits. (2 instances)
Attacks inside final 3km repeatedly fail when peloton speeds remain high and organized. (2 instances)
Final kilometer tactical hesitation costs potential victories when in winning positions. (2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 shows increased late-race aggression and near-victories, but persistent final-kilometer hesitation prevents breakthrough. Domestique role limits consistent opportunities.
